question: What does Angular's default view encapsulation (Emulated) do to your component's CSS?
options: It does nothing — styles are global by default
It uses native browser Shadow DOM to isolate styles
It adds unique attribute selectors (like [_ngcontent-c1]) to your CSS rules and component elements, scoping styles to that component
It converts your CSS to CSS Modules with hashed class names

question: Why is `::ng-deep` considered problematic and marked as deprecated?
options: It has poor browser support and causes layout issues
Styles written with ::ng-deep escape component encapsulation and become global, potentially affecting unrelated components across the app
It only works with ViewEncapsulation.ShadowDom
It prevents Angular from tree-shaking the component's styles

question: You have a *ngFor that renders a 500-item list. What does adding trackBy: trackById do?
options: It sorts the list by id before rendering
It enables virtual scrolling so only visible items render
It gives Angular a stable identity for each item so it can reuse existing DOM elements instead of destroying and recreating the entire list when data changes
It prevents the list from re-rendering when new items are added

question: What is the `@defer (on viewport)` block in Angular 17+?
options: A block that renders conditionally based on a boolean expression
A block that delays rendering AND code-splitting — the deferred component's bundle is not downloaded until the block enters the viewport
An alias for *ngIf with an IntersectionObserver
A block that preloads a component when it is close to the viewport

question: Which CSS selector targets the component's root host element in its own stylesheet?
options: :root {}
.host {}
:host {}
::host {}
